# Figure description: скв_170.jpg

**Source**: `36572_Smolianova_1984/drill_holes_data/скв_170.jpg`  
**Generated by**: claude-sonnet-4-6

---

## Figure Type

This is a **composite borehole/well log (каротажная диаграмма)** — a standard Soviet-era well log sheet combining drilling data ("по бурению") with geophysical well-logging data ("по каротажу"). It includes lithological columns, geophysical curve traces, and a data table with analytical/sample results. A supplementary smaller log panel appears at the bottom of the sheet.

---

## Coverage and Subject

The figure covers a single borehole (скважина). The header area contains identifying information that is partially legible due to scan quality. The well appears to be from the **northern Kazakhstan survey area** referenced in Report 36572. The depth range covered by the main log appears to span from approximately **0 m at surface to roughly 500–600 m depth** based on the scale graduations visible along the left margin. The smaller bottom panel ("контроль" — control/repeat log) covers a shallower interval.

Key identifiers visible in the header include:
- References to **"по бурению"** (drilling data, left side) and **"по каротажу"** (well logging data, right side)
- A title block in the upper portion with what appears to be location/borehole identification text
- A data table occupying the **right-hand portion** of the sheet

---

## Key Content

### Left Section — Drilling Data ("По Бурению"):
- A **lithological column** is shown with standard Soviet geological symbols depicting alternating rock types. Visible patterns include:
  - **Hatched/cross-hatched patterns** in the upper section suggesting crystalline or competent rocks (possibly granites or dense sedimentary rocks)
  - **Dotted patterns** suggesting sandstones or coarser clastic rocks
  - **Horizontal line patterns** suggesting clays, siltstones, or fine-grained sediments
  - **Mixed/interbedded patterns** throughout the middle and lower sections
- Depth markers are graduated along the left margin at regular intervals (likely every 10 m)
- Formation boundary lines are drawn horizontally across the column at several depths
- Several **stratigraphic/formation labels** appear alongside the lithological column, though most are [illegible] at this scan resolution

### Central Section — Geophysical Logs ("По Каротажу"):
Three to four **geophysical curve traces** are plotted as continuous waveform-style lines across the central portion of the sheet. These appear to represent:
1. **КС (кажущееся сопротивление / apparent resistivity)** — one or more resistivity curves showing significant lateral deflections
2. **ПС (потенциал самополяризации / spontaneous potential / SP)** — a smoother curve
3. Possibly **gamma-ray (ГК)** or **neutron logging** curves
4. A **caliper or drilling parameter curve** may also be present

Key observations from the curves:
- Several **high-amplitude resistivity anomalies** are visible, particularly in the middle depth range (~200–350 m), suggesting competent, resistive rock intervals (possibly crystalline basement or dense carbonates)
- The upper ~100 m shows relatively subdued log responses
- Multiple **deflection peaks** in the lower half of the log suggest alternating resistive and conductive intervals
- Annotations near some curve peaks reference values such as **"Ом·м"** scale markings; scale headers at the top indicate log tracks with values visible but partially [illegible]
- Horizontal reference lines and curve annotations at specific depths mark **formation tops or lithological boundaries**

### Right Section — Lithological Column and Data Table:
- A second, interpreted **lithological column** is shown with rock type symbols consistent with the left column
- To the far right, a **multi-column data table** records:
  - Depth intervals (from–to in meters)
  - Sample numbers or core recovery data
  - Analytical results (possibly geochemical or physical property values)
  - Columns with numerical values — most are [illegible] at this resolution but appear to include 1–2 digit numbers and decimal values
  - Column headers are [illegible]

### Bottom Panel — "Контроль" (Control/Repeat Log):
- A smaller repeat or quality-control log is shown at the bottom
- It covers an apparently shallower or selected depth interval
- Three curve traces are visible, consistent with the main log format
- This is standard Soviet practice to verify log quality

---

## Text and Labels

Legible and partially legible text includes:

**Header/Title area:**
- **"ПО БУРЕНИЮ"** (left header — "According to Drilling")
- **"ПО КАРОТАЖУ"** (right header — "According to Well Logging")
- **"ДАТА КАРОТАЖА"** [illegible date] — "Date of Logging"
- **"РЕС."** or similar abbreviation — [illegible]
- **"ЗАБОЙ"** — likely "Bottom of hole" depth reference
- References to **"М-42"** or similar map sheet codes — [illegible at this resolution]

**Curve/Scale labels (top of log tracks):**
- Scale markings visible along the top axis: values such as **"40", "45", "50"** and **"100, 200, 300"** — exact scale units [illegible]
- **"Ом·м"** (Ohm-metres — resistivity units) likely present
- **"мВ"** (millivolts — SP units) likely present

**Annotations on curves:**
- **"Ом·м-180"** or similar annotation at a prominent resistivity peak — [partially illegible]
- **"Ср.н"** or similar abbreviation near curve features — [illegible]
- Depth annotations at formation boundaries, approximately at depths: **~100 m, ~200 m, ~280 m, ~380 m, ~480 m** (approximate, based on scale)

**Stratigraphic labels (left column):**
- **"С₂₋₃"** or similar Carboniferous age designations — [partially visible]
- **"Д"** or **"С"** formation codes — [illegible]

**Bottom panel label:**
- **"КОНТРОЛЬ"** — "Control" (repeat/verification log)

**Bottom-right title block:**
- Contains borehole identification, organization name
